framework_name: FFIEC IT Examination Handbook — Audit booklet

controls.title: Audit Charter and Independence
controls.description: The internal audit function operates under a board-approved charter establishing organizational independence + reporting to the board / audit committee.

controls.title: Audit Committee Oversight
controls.description: The audit committee (or equivalent) approves the audit plan, reviews findings, and oversees remediation of identified issues.

controls.title: Auditor Qualifications + Continuing Education
controls.description: Audit staff possess appropriate IT + financial-services qualifications + complete annual continuing education to maintain competency.

controls.title: External Audit Coordination
controls.description: Internal audit coordinates with external auditors + regulatory examiners to avoid duplication + maximize coverage efficiency.

controls.title: Risk-Based Audit Planning
controls.description: The audit plan is built on a risk assessment covering all significant IT processes + systems, prioritized by business criticality + risk magnitude.

controls.title: Annual Audit Plan Coverage
controls.description: The annual audit plan covers IT governance, infrastructure, applications, information security, business continuity, third-party relationships, + IT operations on a documented cadence.

controls.title: Audit Universe Maintenance
controls.description: The audit universe (the population of auditable units) is documented + refreshed at least annually to reflect new systems, processes, + third-party relationships.

controls.title: Resource Allocation + Budget
controls.description: Audit resources (headcount, budget, tooling) are sized to deliver the approved audit plan within the planned cycle.

controls.title: Multi-Year Coverage Strategy
controls.description: High-risk areas receive at-least-annual coverage; medium-risk every 2-3 years; low-risk on a longer cycle. Coverage gaps are documented with rationale.

controls.title: Audit Programs + Procedures
controls.description: Engagement-specific audit programs document scope, objectives, test procedures, + sample-selection methodology.

controls.title: Workpaper Documentation
controls.description: Audit workpapers document the procedures performed, evidence obtained, + conclusions reached, with sufficient detail for an independent reviewer to replicate the work.

controls.title: Use of CAATs (Computer-Assisted Audit Techniques)
controls.description: Auditors use data-analytic + sampling tooling to test transaction-level controls + identify anomalies at scale.

controls.title: Quality-Assurance + Peer Review
controls.description: Audit work undergoes documented supervisory + peer review before issuance of the final report.

controls.title: Audit Sampling Methodology
controls.description: Statistical or judgmental sampling methods are documented + applied consistently; sample sizes are justified relative to control population + assurance objective.

controls.title: Findings Classification + Severity
controls.description: Audit findings are classified by severity (high / moderate / low) using documented criteria + reported to appropriate management levels.

controls.title: Management Response + Remediation Commitments
controls.description: Each finding receives a documented management response with remediation owner, target date, + commitment to closure.

controls.title: Audit Report Distribution
controls.description: Audit reports are distributed to appropriate levels: high-severity findings to the audit committee + senior management; lower findings to relevant operating management.

controls.title: Trend Analysis + Periodic Reporting
controls.description: Aggregate audit results + trends are reported to the audit committee on at least an annual basis.

controls.title: Issue Management System
controls.description: All audit findings are tracked in a documented system showing status, target date, owner, + evidence of closure.

controls.title: Validation of Remediation
controls.description: Audit independently validates that management's remediation actions effectively address the underlying control gap before closing the finding.

controls.title: Aging + Escalation
controls.description: Past-due findings escalate through documented thresholds; findings that age substantially past target receive board / audit committee attention.

controls.title: Repeat Findings Analysis
controls.description: Repeat findings (same control gap re-emerging) are tracked + analyzed for systemic root cause; chronic repeats trigger management-level remediation.

controls.title: Information Security Audit
controls.description: IS audits cover access control, encryption, vulnerability + patch management, incident response, + security monitoring controls.

controls.title: Business Continuity + DR Audit
controls.description: BC/DR audits cover plan documentation, BIA accuracy, recovery testing, + alignment with the institution's RTO + RPO commitments.

controls.title: Third-Party / Vendor Audit
controls.description: Critical vendors receive periodic audit coverage via on-site visits, SOC report review, or right-to-audit exercises.

controls.title: IT Project Audit
controls.description: Material IT projects (e.g., core-banking conversions, M&A system integrations) receive audit coverage at key project milestones.

controls.title: Cloud + SaaS Audit
controls.description: Cloud + SaaS arrangements are audited for shared-responsibility model clarity, vendor SOC report sufficiency, + data-residency compliance.

controls.title: Application + Change Management Audit
controls.description: Application development + change management processes are audited for SDLC adherence, segregation-of-duties, + production-change controls.

controls.title: Data Governance + Privacy Audit
controls.description: Data governance (classification, retention, access) + privacy (GLBA, state laws) processes receive audit coverage on a documented cadence.

controls.title: Use of Continuous Auditing Techniques
controls.description: Where appropriate, the audit function deploys continuous-auditing tooling to detect control failures in near-real-time rather than periodic-only review.

controls.title: Audit Skill + Tooling Investment
controls.description: The audit function invests in skills (cloud / cyber / data analytics) + tooling (CAATs, GRC platforms) to keep pace with the institution's technology + risk profile.

controls.title: External Quality Assessment
controls.description: The audit function obtains an external quality assessment (typically every 5 years per IIA standards) confirming conformance with applicable professional standards.
